exemplar.title: To what extent does the creativity of the marketing campaign of the Barbie movie lead it to domestic marketing success (United States and Canada)?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the creativity of the marketing campaign of the Barbie movie lead it to domestic marketing success (United States and Canada)?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies and repeatedly returns to the concept of creativity throughout the analysis
Demonstrates how creative tactics (pink billboards, selfie generator) drove audience engagement
Links creativity explicitly to quantitative findings (margin of safety)
Applies the concept to multiple tools (SWOT, perceptual map, break-even)
weaknesses: Occasional long narrative lists lack deeper conceptual critique
SWOT introduction does not explicitly tie each item back to creativity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in Band 4: the key concept is well understood and applied in most sections, showing clear analytical thinking and strong organizational linkage. Occasional drift into description prevents full integration (Band 5), so a score of 4/5 best reflects the evidence.

strengths: Exactly five supporting documents provided, meeting quantity requirements
Sources (box-office databases, industry case studies, Warner Bros. site) are directly relevant to campaign impact
Documents feed into revenue, social-media, and tactic analyses
weaknesses: Lacks diversity of perspectives (no academic or financial filings)
Several citations are repeated without adding new insights
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Quantity reaches the top band (Band 4), but the relevance and depth plateau at Band 3 because, while sufficiently in-depth, the range of ideas is limited. A score of 3/4 balances these observations.

strengths: Methodology table clearly lays out why SWOT, perceptual map, and break-even were chosen
Provides a balanced qualitativeâquantitative toolkit adequate for the research question
weaknesses: Break-even calculation uses implausible ticket prices and inconsistent units
Perceptualâmap axes lack rationale and depth
SWOT analysis remains largely descriptive without prioritization
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tool selection is adequate (Band 3) but application shows superficial relevance (Band 2). Overall performance sits between these, justifying a score of 3/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses box-office figures, budget estimates, and calculated break-even metrics appropriately
Narrative links creative tactics to performance outcomes
Tables and charts support data points adequately
weaknesses: Some data points lack realism (ticket-price units)
Analysis sometimes asserts causal links without full evidence
Units and scale inconsistencies dilute clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analysis are adequate (both Band 3). The work shows genuine analytical effort but is uneven and not fully sustained, warranting 3/5.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, affirming creativityâs vital role
Restates key findings in alignment with major evidence
weaknesses: Calculation errors and dataâreliability concerns weaken consistency
Conclusion underplays tool limitations and suggests no further research
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are consistent with evidence only to a moderate degree (Band 2), even though the research question is fully addressed (Band 3). Overall, a score of 2/3 reflects these mixed strands.

strengths: Logical sequence of sections with clear headings and subheadings
Includes table of contents and IB formatting compliance
weaknesses: Table and figure captions sometimes duplicate labels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organization is appropriate and clear throughout (Band 2). Minor formatting lapses prevent a Band 1 deduction but do not undermine overall structure.

strengths: All required IB elements are present (word count, RQ, key concept, supporting documents list)
Visuals, tables, and bibliography are included and correctly formatted overall
weaknesses: Placeholder math markup in TOC disrupts flow
Figure labels lack dot leaders for easy navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets IB requirements (Band 2) despite minor stylistic and navigational issues. A topâband score of 2/2 is justified.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical sequence (introduction, methodology, tools, conclusion) and provides tables of contents and figures.  Headings and sub-headings are clear, though some duplication (two â3â headings) and stray formatting reduce polish.  Overall structure is appropriate throughout, warranting Band 2.

The five documents are drawn from box-office databases, industry case studies and Warner Bros.â own site â all relevant to measuring campaign impact.  They feed directly into revenue, social-media and campaign-tactic evidence, giving sufficient depth for each tool.  

However, coverage is somewhat narrow (all sources focus on popular-media commentary; no academic or financial filings are used) and several citations are repeated within the narrative without fresh insights.  Thus the material is ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than offering the ârange of ideasâ expected for the top band.

All required IB elements are present: word count, RQ, key concept, supporting documents list, and consistent citation style.  Visuals, tables and bibliography are included.  Therefore the highest band for presentation is earned.

Exactly five documents are listed under âsupporting documentsâ and clearly labelled SD#1-SD#5, matching the rubric requirement for 3-5 documents.  No excess materials dilute the set, so the top band for quantity is justified.

Application shows clear intent but contains weaknesses:
â¢ Break-even uses implausible ticket prices ($0.00001) and mixes âmillionâ labels with unit calculations, signalling superficial numerical understanding.
â¢ The perceptual map plots only two films and offers limited axis justification.
â¢ SWOT points are largely descriptive with minimal weighting or prioritisation.

Thus relevance to the research question is shown but remains superficial; Band 2 (âsuperficial relevanceâ) best reflects this level.

Box-office revenue, marketing-budget estimates, social-media sentiment percentages and calculated break-even figures provide adequate quantitative support.  Data are generally sourced and embedded in tables and charts, satisfying âadequate selection and useâ.  Some data (e.g., unit ticket price) lack realism, preventing classification as âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ; therefore Band 3 is most realistic.

The narrative explains how creative tactics boosted brand engagement, how rivalry with Oppenheimer created viral momentum, and how revenue exceeded the break-even point.  These passages constitute genuine analysis but are uneven: causal chains are sometimes asserted rather than evidenced, and evaluation of weaknesses is brief.  Integration across tools exists but is not fully sustained, matching âsome analysis of the research questionâ at Band 3.

The student explicitly answers the research question, declaring that creativity âplayed a vital roleâ in domestic success and qualifying the extent with references to short-term buzz.  This direct, reasoned answer meets the top-band descriptor for this strand.

Three standard business tools (SWOT, perceptual map, break-even) are selected and justified in the methodology table, covering qualitative positioning and quantitative costârevenue analysis.  This represents an adequate, balanced toolkit for the research question, but it is not especially wide or innovative.  Hence Band 3 (âadequate selectionâ) is most fitting.

The conclusion restates key findings and generally aligns with evidence presented, but calculation errors and limited critique of data reliability undermine full consistency.  Consequently, the strand satisfies âsome conclusions consistent with evidenceâ (Band 2) rather than full alignment.

The investigation remains consistently anchored to Warner Bros.â Barbie campaign and its domestic performance.  Each analytical tool is populated with organization-specific evidence: box-office revenue, marketing-budget figures, examples of the selfie generator and pink billboards, and a direct comparison with the rival film Oppenheimer.  

Links between these data points and the firmâs marketing objectives are drawn (e.g., how collaborations âsolidified a cultural movementâ, how an inflated $150 m budget created ROI pressure).  This shows clear and largely integrated connection to the organization rather than a generic application.

Minor lapses existâsome figures (ticket price $0.00001) are implausible and weaken credibilityâso the strand stops short of the âcomprehensive throughoutâ requirement of Band 5; Band 4 is therefore most accurate.

annotations.comment: The table of contents shows inconsistent numbering with two main sections labeled â3,â which may confuse readers. Consider revising to maintain unique, hierarchical headings for clarity.
Including placeholder math markup in the TOC (e.g., â2 | P ageâ) disrupts readability. Remove or correct this to maintain professional presentation.
Figure listings are comprehensive, but the lack of page alignment or dot leaders weakens navigation. Add consistent dot leaders to link figure titles to page numbers.
The introduction succinctly defines the research question and aligns with IB formatting requirements, demonstrating clear structure from the outset.
The definition of creativity is accurate but unlinked to Warner Bros.â context. Integrate examples of campaign creativity here to anchor the concept to the organization.
The methodology table clearly labels chosen tools and purposes, providing a wellâstructured overview of qualitative and quantitative approaches.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Justification for tool selection remains brief; strengthen by explaining why SWOT, perceptual mapping, and breakâeven are especially effective for assessing creativityâs impact.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: SWOT diagram presents clear categories but lists are descriptive without prioritization. Assign weights or rank factors to elevate analytical depth.
Strength analysis effectively describes brandârecognition tactics, with vivid examples of pink billboards. Consider citing engagement metrics to support claims.
Crossâplatform integration is noted, but the text reads as narrative rather than critical analysis. Discuss the degree of audience engagement achieved across platforms.
The weakness bullet uses verbose language and generalizations. Tighten by focusing on how nostalgiaâdriven creativity limits demographic reach with concrete data.
Opportunity analysis highlights âBarbenheimerâ buzz well, but could be enriched by quantifying social media reach or viral impressions.
The threats section insightfully critiques shortâterm hype but lacks postâlaunch engagement data. Incorporate metrics on traffic dropâoff to substantiate sustainability concerns.
Budgetârisk bullet cites $150 million but does not relate it to ROI. Discuss breakeven pressure numerically to strengthen the argument.
The perceptual map diagram spans both films but lacks axis rationale. Label axes clearly and explain why those dimensions measure creativity and success.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The SWOT commentary introduction offers a basic rationale but lacks explicit connection to creativity. Link each SWOT item back to how it reflects the key concept.
Analysis of the perception map names general trends but avoids specific score differentials. Cite the data points plotted for more rigorous evaluation.
Sales and quantity figures use inconsistent units (e.g., 63.5 $). Correct to millions of tickets to reflect accurate scale and avoid confusion.
The labeled ticket price of $0.00001 is implausible. Recalculate with realistic perâticket revenue (e.g., $10) to maintain credibility.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The breakâeven chart table includes many data points, but a graphical representation with clear breakeven line would better illustrate the intersection point.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Table captions like âTable 3: break even chart data (2)â lack differentiation. Use distinct labels or merge data sources to avoid duplication.
The breakeven calculation uses fixed cost of $245 million but earlier table shows $245. Ensure unit consistency when applying the formula: $$\text{BEP} = \frac{\text{Fixed Cost}}{P - VC}$$.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Graph 1 visually supports the analysis, but the axes lack clear labels and units (tickets vs. millions). Add these for immediate comprehension.
The breakâeven analysis interpretation accurately calculates margin of safety (29.6%) and links to creativityâs impact, demonstrating a strong connection to the key concept.
Conclusions restate findings but underplay tool limitations. Expand critique on data reliability and suggest further research to demonstrate evaluative depth.
Supporting document list correctly includes five items, meeting rubric requirements, but the distinction between supporting and supplementary documents could be more explicit.
The bibliography entries exhibit minor inconsistencies in formatting (bolding SD numbers vs. plain text). Standardize citation style for professional presentation.
